---
title: "Initial Deepflora results"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Accuracy Metrics

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
metricsdir <- "results/accuracy_metrics/"
metricfiles <- list.files(metricsdir, full.names = TRUE)
beeplants <- readRDS("data/beeplants.rds")

overall <- lapply(metricfiles[grep("overall_results", metricfiles)], read.csv)
names(overall) <- strsplit(basename(metricfiles[grep("overall_results", metricfiles)]), "_") |> sapply(\(x) unlist(x)[1])

perobs <- lapply(metricfiles[grep("observations", metricfiles)], read.csv)
names(perobs) <- strsplit(basename(metricfiles[grep("observations", metricfiles)]), "_") |> sapply(\(x) unlist(x)[1])

persp <- lapply(metricfiles[grep("species", metricfiles)], read.csv)
names(persp) <- strsplit(basename(metricfiles[grep("species", metricfiles)]), "_") |> sapply(\(x) unlist(x)[1])

make_persp_long <- function(x) {
  x |> 
    dplyr::select(-dset_name, -model, -loss, -exp_id, -pretrained, -date, -batch_size, -epoch, -thres, -band) |> 
  column_to_rownames("metric") |>
  t() |> as.data.frame() |>
  rownames_to_column("scientificName") |> 
  mutate(scientificName = gsub("\\."," ",scientificName)) %>%
  separate_wider_delim(scientificName, delim=" ",
                       names=c("genus","sp"),
                       too_few = "align_start",
                       too_many = "merge",
                       cols_remove = FALSE)
}

persp.l <- lapply(persp, make_persp_long)

# all bee floral resource sp (Grozinger + Fowler)
# persp.fr <- persp.l %>%
#   filter(genus %in% beeplants$genus$genus |
#            scientificName %in% beeplants$species$scientificName)

# all PA bee floral resource sp (Grozinger list)
persp.fr <- lapply(persp.l, \(x){
    filter(x, genus %in% beeplants$genus$genus |
           scientificName %in% (beeplants$species %>% 
                                  dplyr::filter(source == "Grozinger") %>% 
                                  pull(scientificName)))
})

# plotting df
plot_persp <- list(full=bind_rows(persp.l, .id = "model"), floralres=bind_rows(persp.fr, .id = "model")) |>
  bind_rows(.id = "subset") |>
  pivot_longer(species_top1:support, names_to = "metric")

```

Plot results comparison
```{r}
library(rstatix)
library(ggsignif)

model_labels <- c(
  "initial" = "Deepbiosphere",
  # "bioclimmlp" = "Bioclim MLP",
  # "tresnet" = "Remote sensing TResNet",
  "maxent" = "Maxent",
  "rf" = "Random Forest"
)

# Map your metric column values to display labels
metric_labels <- c(
  "calibrated_PRC_AUC" = "calibrated~AUC[PRC]",
  "calibrated_ROC_AUC" = "calibrated~AUC[ROC]",
  "f1_score"           = "F1~score",
  "PRC_AUC"            = "AUC[PRC]",
  "precision_score"    = "Precision~score",
  "recall_score"       = "Recall~score",
  "ROC_AUC"            = "AUC[ROC]",
  "species_top1"       = "Top~'1'[spp]",
  "species_top100"     = "Top~'100'[spp]",
  "species_top30"      = "Top~'30'[spp]",
  "species_top5"       = "Top~'5'[spp]"
)

# Desired panel order (matches reading order in the figure)
metric_order <- c(
  "calibrated_PRC_AUC", "calibrated_ROC_AUC",
  "ROC_AUC",  "PRC_AUC", "precision_score",   "recall_score", "f1_score",
  "species_top1", "species_top5","species_top30","species_top100")

df <- plot_persp %>%
  filter(metric %in% metric_order) %>%
  mutate(
    model  = factor(model, levels = names(model_labels)),
    metric = factor(metric, levels = metric_order)
  )

stat_results_full <- df %>% dplyr::filter(subset == "full") %>%
  arrange(metric, scientificName, model) %>%  # same species order for every model
  group_by(subset, metric) %>%
  wilcox_test(
    value ~ model,
    ref.group   = "initial",
    paired      = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"        # correct for multiple comparisons within metric
  ) %>%
  add_significance(
    p.col = "p.adj",
    cutpoints = c(0, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 1),
    symbols   = c("***", "**", "*", "NS.")
  ) %>%
  add_xy_position(
    x     = "model",
    dodge = 0.8,
    # step.increase = 0.07   # ggsignif needs y positions for bracket heights
    scales = "fixed"
  )

stat_results_fr <- df %>% dplyr::filter(subset == "floralres") %>%
  arrange(metric, scientificName, model) %>%  # same species order for every model
  group_by(subset, metric) %>%
  wilcox_test(
    value ~ model,
    ref.group   = "initial",
    paired      = TRUE,
    p.adjust.method = "BH"        # correct for multiple comparisons within metric
  ) %>%
  add_significance(
    p.col = "p.adj",
    cutpoints = c(0, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 1),
    symbols   = c("***", "**", "*", "NS.")
  ) %>%
  add_xy_position(
    x     = "model",
    dodge = 0.8,
    # step.increase = 0.07   # ggsignif needs y positions for bracket heights
    scales = "fixed"
  )

medians <- df %>%
  group_by(subset,metric, model) %>%
  summarise(med = round(median(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3), .groups = "drop")
means <- df %>%
  group_by(subset,metric, model) %>%
  summarise(mean = round(mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), 3), .groups = "drop")

pfull <- ggplot(df %>% dplyr::filter(subset == "full"), 
            aes(x = model, y = value, fill = model, color = model)) +

  geom_violin(alpha = 0.35, trim = FALSE, scale = "width", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, size = 0.6, alpha = 0.25, shape = 16) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0,
               color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = means %>% filter(subset == "full"),
    aes(x = model, y = mean, label = mean),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.5
  ) +
  # Use precomputed stats instead of computing inside geom_signif
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(
    stat_results_full,
    label         = "p.adj.signif",   # uses the *** / NS. symbols
    tip.length    = 0.01,
    step.increase = 0.07,
    size          = 2.5,
    step.group.by = "metric",
    hide.ns       = FALSE             # set TRUE to drop NS. brackets
  ) +
  # scale_fill_manual(values  = model_colors) +
  # scale_color_manual(values = model_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.4),   # extra headroom for brackets
                     breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00)) +

  facet_wrap(~ metric,
             labeller = as_labeller(metric_labels, label_parsed),
             ncol = 3
             ) +

  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    axis.text.x        = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.title         = element_blank(),
    strip.background   = element_blank(),
    strip.text         = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

pfr <- ggplot(df %>% dplyr::filter(subset == "floralres"), 
            aes(x = model, y = value, fill = model, color = model)) +

  geom_violin(alpha = 0.35, trim = FALSE, scale = "width", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.08, size = 0.6, alpha = 0.25, shape = 16) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0,
               color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(
    data = means %>% filter(subset == "floralres"),
    aes(x = model, y = mean, label = mean),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.2, fontface = "bold", vjust = -0.5
  ) +
  # Use precomputed stats instead of computing inside geom_signif
  ggpubr::stat_pvalue_manual(
    stat_results_fr,
    label         = "p.adj.signif",   # uses the *** / NS. symbols
    tip.length    = 0.01,
    step.increase = 0.01,
    size          = 2.5,
    step.group.by = "metric",
    hide.ns       = FALSE             # set TRUE to drop NS. brackets
  ) +
  # scale_fill_manual(values  = model_colors) +
  # scale_color_manual(values = model_colors) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.4),   # extra headroom for brackets
                     breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1.00)) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric,
             labeller = as_labeller(metric_labels, label_parsed),
             ncol = 3,
             ) +

  theme_bw(base_size = 9) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    axis.text.x        = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 7),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.title         = element_blank(),
    strip.background   = element_blank(),
    strip.text         = element_text(size = 8, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

```
